title: Implementing Regulation on the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism
bodyHtml:
The European Commission has published the Regulation on the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) Implementation, aimed at supporting and guiding the transitional period applications of BCAM, which will run from October 1, 2023, to December 31, 2025.

Before its acceptance by the Commission, the Implementation Regulation was presented to the public for their opinions and was approved by the CBAM Committee consisting of representatives from European Union (EU) member states.

The Implementation Regulation provides detailed methodologies for calculating embedded emissions during the production of products covered by the regulation.

During the CBAM transition process, the Commission has also released an Implementation Guide to address practical aspects of the application, providing support to importers, exporters, and customs consultants. Furthermore, specialized information technology tools have been developed to assist importing firms in their calculation and reporting processes. Various training materials have been prepared, and online seminars will be organized to facilitate this process.

CBAM will initially be applied to high-carbon-emission products in the sectors of iron and steel, aluminum, cement, fertilizers, electricity, and hydrogen during its initial phase. Importers will be obligated to report greenhouse gas emissions produced during the manufacturing process of goods without incurring any financial obligations. The data collected during this process will play a role in defining the final rules for CBAM implementation after 2026.

For the rules concerning reporting obligations during the transition period, please refer to the Implementation Regulation governing CBAM.

The EU Commission has also prepared guidelines for the transition period.

For the guidelines intended for businesses exporting to the EU, click here.

For the EU Commission's page on the  Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism, including relevant guides, click here.
